content.text: can you take a look at https://github.com/entireio/git-sync/security/dependabot/5
I'll take a look at that Dependabot alert.
That's an authenticated URL, so I'll use the `gh` CLI to fetch the alert details.

content.text: Here's what alert #5 is about, and let me check where we stand.

input.command: echo "=== go.mod x/net ===" && grep "golang.org/x/net" go.mod; echo "=== go.sum x/net ===" && grep "golang.org/x/net" go.sum | head; echo "=== is it direct or via what? ===" && go mod why golang.org/x/net 2>&1 | head -30
input.description: Check x/net version and dependency chain

result.output:

# golang.org/x/net
(main module does not need package golang.org/x/net)

input.command: echo "=== what pulls x/net ===" && go mod graph 2>/dev/null | grep "golang.org/x/net@" | head -20; echo "=== do we import x/net/html anywhere ===" && grep -rn "x/net/html" --include="*.go" . | head; echo "=== go version ===" && go version

content.text:
## Dependabot alert #5 summary

| | |
|---|---|
| **Package** | `golang.org/x/net` (transitive) |
| **Advisory** | GHSA-5cv4-jp36-h3mw / CVE-2026-25680 |
| **Severity** | Medium (CVSS 6.5) |
| **Issue** | The HTML parser (`x/net/html`) can consume excessive CPU on crafted input → denial of service (CWE-400) |
| **Affected** | `< 0.55.0` — we have **v0.54.0** |
| **Fix** | Bump to **v0.55.0** |

**Exposure assessment:** This is low real-world risk for us:
- `x/net` is a purely **transitive** dependency (`go mod why` says the main module doesn't even need it — it comes in via `go-git/v6` and `x/crypto`).
- The vulnerability is specifically in `x/net/html`, and we **don't import that package anywhere** in the codebase.

So we're not actually exercising the vulnerable code path — but the clean fix is still just a version bump. Let me apply it:

input.command: go get golang.org/x/net@v0.55.0 && go mod tidy && echo "=== result ===" && grep "golang.org/x/net" go.mod

result.output:
go: upgraded golang.org/x/net v0.54.0 => v0.55.0
go: upgraded golang.org/x/sys v0.44.0 => v0.45.0
=== result ===
	golang.org/x/net v0.55.0 // indirect

input.command: go build ./... && echo "BUILD OK" && git --no-pager diff --stat
input.description: Verify build and show changed files

result.output:
BUILD OK
 go.mod | 4 ++--
 go.sum | 8 ++++----
 2 files changed, 6 insertions(+), 6 deletions(-)

content.text:
Done. Here's the outcome:

**Fixed alert #5** by bumping `golang.org/x/net` **v0.54.0 → v0.55.0** (which also pulled `x/sys` v0.44.0 → v0.45.0). Build passes.

Changes are staged in the working tree only (`go.mod` + `go.sum`) — I haven't committed. Note we're currently on branch `soph/enable-ref-backend`.

Want me to:
- **Commit** this on the current branch, or a fresh branch (e.g. `soph/bump-x-net`)?
- Open a **PR**?

Also worth knowing: since `x/net` is only a transitive dep and we don't touch `x/net/html`, there was no real exposure — this bump just clears the Dependabot alert cleanly.
do a frech branch but base it of this branch

input.command:
git checkout -b soph/bump-x-net && git add go.mod go.sum && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Bump golang.org/x/net to v0.55.0 to fix CVE-2026-25680

Clears Dependabot alert #5 (GHSA-5cv4-jp36-h3mw). The HTML parser in
x/net < 0.55.0 can consume excessive CPU on crafted input (CWE-400).
x/net is a transitive dependency and we don't import x/net/html, so
there was no real exposure, but this bump clears the alert cleanly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && echo "=== log ===" && git --no-pager log --oneline -3
input.description: Create branch off current and commit the bump

 2 files changed, 6 insertions(+), 6 deletions(-)

content.text:
Done. Created branch **`soph/bump-x-net`** off `soph/enable-ref-backend` and committed the bump (`770216b1`).

Want me to push it and open a PR? If so, against which base — `soph/enable-ref-backend` or `main`?
